###### OBITUARY

# James Drayton Smith Jr.

###### March 8, 1944 – July 14, 2020

![Obituary of James Drayton Smith Jr.](https://dux7id0k7hacn.cloudfront.net/as/assets-mem-com/cmi/3/1/4/2/9262413/20200716_104431077_0_orig.jpg/-/james-smith-birmingham-al-obituary.jpg?a.balancewhite=true&crop=%2854.404296875%2C51.95833333333333%2C539.20703125%2C462.79166666666663%29&cropxunits=619&cropyunits=464&maxheight=650)

IN THE CARE OF

[**Johns-Ridout's Mortuary-Elmwood Chapel & Elmwood Cemetery & Mausoleum**](https://www.dignitymemorial.com/funeral-homes/alabama/birmingham/johns-ridouts-mortuary-elmwood-chapel/4391)

James D. Smith, Jr. passed away on July 14, 2020. He was born on March 8, 1944 in Birmingham, AL to James Drayton Smith, Sr. and Vernzola McCarley Smith, both of whom preceded him in death. He is survived by his sons, James Drayton Smith, III (Mallory) and Charles Benjamin Smith (Ginny); grandchildren, Tucker Drayton Smith, Mary Elizabeth Smith, Tanner Blaine Smith, Charles Benjamin Smith, Jr., Stanford Wood Smith, and Gunner Huckleberry Smith; former wife, Sharon Scarbrough Smith; sister Deborah Smith Fulford; and niece Smith Fulford Sherrer (Matt).

Jim attended Shades Valley High School, Auburn University (Delta Chi Fraternity), and UAB School of Dentistry (Class of 1984). He enjoyed his patients and the practice of dentistry for 33 years in the Inverness area. Jim loved the beach, his automobiles, and watching his grand kids grow up. He loved people and interacting with his countless friends.

The family will receive friends on Friday, July 17, 2020 at 10am at Elmwood Chapel Funeral Home. A celebration of Jim's life will follow in the chapel at 11am. Due to the ordinance from Jefferson County, facemasks are required; as well as safe social distancing.

In lieu of flowers, please consider making a donation to the Lewy Body Dementia Association, 912 Killian Hill Road S.W., Lilburn, GA 30047, or by clicking the link below. [https://www.lbda.org/donate](https://www.lbda.org/donate)

[![I was a patient of Dr. Smith's for 17 years.  There was no other quite like him!](https://d2mjvz2lqjkhe7.cloudfront.net/as/assets-mem-com/cmi/3/1/4/2/9262413/Condolences/d6565741-6a4d-4389-8add-9610c21b0025_orig.jpg/-/james-smith-birmingham-al-memories27048458.jpg)](javascript:;)

I was a patient of Dr. Smith's for 17 years. There was no other quite like him!

### Marilyn Weiss

01/30/2024

I was so saddened to hear of Dr. James Smith's passing. I moved away from B'ham, AL in 2012 and always wondered how he was doing. Now I know.
One of my greatest pleasures in life was the reward of his dental work, I considered him an "Artist". Needless to say I have had a tremendous amount of dental work done by him. Truly, he made me smile even to this day! Years later to this very day other dentists look at the work Dr. Smith did on my teeth & rave what fine work he did. I treasure his memory. He certainly made a wonderful difference in my life. I will always think of him with fond memories. I miss him. \*\*\* photo sent so you could see his dental work through the years. IT took a lot!!
Lovingly, Marilyn (Mary) Weiss. (formerly of B'ham, AL & now Midland, MI)

0SHARE

Add a Comment

### Sherry Smirh

07/18/2020

I am so sorry for your families loss and prayers that God will give you all peace and comfort for the days ahead. I personally didn’t know Jimmy, but felt like I did. My friend, Carol Sue (cousin) talked about Jimmy a lot when I would do her hair. I was Carol’s hairdresser for over 20 years.
She loved Jimmy and thought the world of him. He was truly her favorite cousin. She was so excited the year she got to go visit and see all his Christmas decorations and fun stuff. I smile because I can see them both in Heaven laughing and cutting up. I know Carol welcomed him into Heaven, along side of Jesus.

Praying for your family,
Sherry Smith
Pace, FL

0SHARE

Add a Comment

### Judy and Richard Holland

07/16/2020

I, Judy (Smith) Holland, join our many cousins that remember our "loving first cousin", Jimmy, who always wore a beautiful, wide smile on his face, and brought humor, warmth, and joy to every occasion we shared together. Sharon, children, and grandchildren, we are extremely saddened to learn of Jimmy's death, but also thrilled to picture him with his Parents and all family members who preceded him in death, as they join together in Worshiping, serving, and Praising Jesus. My sister, Carol Sue, must be extremely gleeful as she welcomes Jimmy into heaven with her!
With grief and love,
Judy and Richard Holland
